textPlain: According to the discoverer, NVISO: “We can however not assess whether this exploit was part of UNC5174’s capabilities or whether the zero-day’s usage was merely accidental due to its trivialness.”This may explain the failure to notify of a 0day, since it seemed to be exploited accidentally in the course of a more sophisticated operation.But that doesn’t excuse the lack of disclosure IMO. If it’s so trivial you could accidentally exploit it, seems bad.
That's going to cost them big time in the EU when the CRA goes into full effect in a couple years.  Theoretically anyway.
